subject: Public Health
title: Substance Use Disorders
description: A fast breakdown of Substance Use Disorders for builders.

hook.text: Addiction hijacks the brain's reward system—turning a choice into a compulsion that overrides reason.

buildup.text: Drugs flood the brain with dopamine—up to 10 times the normal amount—rewiring pleasure circuits.

discovery.text: Over 35 million people worldwide suffer from substance use disorders—yet only 1 in 7 receives treatment.

twist.text: Addiction is now recognized as a chronic brain disorder—not a moral failing or lack of willpower.

climax.text: Harm reduction approaches—like needle exchanges and supervised sites—save lives while people recover.

punchline.text: Addiction changes the brain. Recovery changes it back.

quiz.question: What fraction of people with substance use disorders receives treatment?
